justification: All 6 rules classified correctly (BR-1: true, BR-2: false, BR-3: false, BR-4: true, BR-5: false, BR-6: true). Each rule has a detailed written justification that clearly distinguishes state integrity constraints from configurable policy parameters. Edge cases and escalations go well beyond minimum requirements.

trials.justification: All 6 rules classified correctly: BR-1 (true), BR-2 (false), BR-3 (false), BR-4 (true), BR-5 (false), BR-6 (true). Each rule has detailed written justification clearly distinguishing state integrity constraints from configurable policy parameters. Rules 2, 3, and 5 explicitly use 'configurable'/'tunable' reasoning. The grace period ambiguity is surfaced in multiple places — escalations cover tier-change interaction, and edge cases note the premium upgrade mid-failure scenario. observations.text: Perfect 6/6 classification accuracy with no misclassifications

No explicit standalone recommendation to 'store policies as configuration' — the concept is embedded in justifications but not called out as an architectural directive
